title: Facets, Calendars, Sessions, Pagination, Search, and Infinite URL Spaces · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs facets, calendars, sessions, pagination, search, and infinite url spaces from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for URL-space trap detector and budget policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Detect repeated filter dimensions, permutations, empty states, sort variants, and low-value combinations using explicit site knowledge.
Bound date ranges, next and previous traversal, generated archives, and cyclic calendar links against stakeholder time scope.
Identify session, tracking, nonce, cache-buster, and personalized parameters without deleting unknown keys by guesswork.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 1221 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to detect faceted, calendar, session, pagination, search, and query-space traps before resource budgets fail; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure discovery growth, unique-content yield, rejection ratios, queue age, and owner-defined value to stop expanding traps early. Artifact under review: URL-space trap detector and budget policy.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
